tool.name: Keep success

param.name: replacement
param.label: Replace unsuccessful elements with this dataset
param.help: If provided, unsuccessful elements will be replaced with this dataset

tool.help:
======== Synopsis ========
Keeps only datasets in the success (green) state from a collection.
=========== Description ===========
This tool takes a dataset collection and retains only elements whose datasets are in the success (green/ok) state. Elements in any other state — failed (red), paused (gray), or still running — are removed from the output collection.
This is stricter than **Filter failed datasets**, which only removes elements in the error state. **Keep success** removes *anything* that is not ok, including paused datasets that have not yet completed.
This is useful when a multi-sample workflow has partially completed and you want to continue with just the samples that succeeded, discarding any that failed or stalled.
Optionally, you can provide a **replacement dataset**. When specified, unsuccessful elements are replaced with that dataset instead of being removed. This preserves the collection size, which is important when element-wise pairing with another collection is needed.
======== Examples ========
**Remove unsuccessful elements**
Given a collection where one sample failed and another is paused::
List: "Aligned reads"
sample1 → aligned.bam (ok) sample2 → aligned.bam (error) sample3 → aligned.bam (ok) sample4 → aligned.bam (paused)
The output keeps only the successful elements::
List: "Aligned reads (only successful datasets)"
sample1 → aligned.bam sample3 → aligned.bam
-------
**Replace instead of remove**
If a replacement dataset is provided, unsuccessful elements are swapped rather than dropped::
List: "Aligned reads (only successful datasets)"
sample1 → aligned.bam sample2 → replacement.bam sample3 → aligned.bam sample4 → replacement.bam
-------
.. image:: ${static_path}/images/tools/collection_ops/keep_success.svg :alt: Keep only successful datasets in a collection :width: 500
-------
.. class:: infomark
@QUOTA_USAGE_NOTE@
